title: WCAG 2.2 AA + plain-language readability hardening (automated + manual AT audit)
context: WCAG 2.2 AA and plain-language readability are hard gates for this project, not optional improvements. The intended beneficiaries include low-literacy readers, non-specialist caregivers, renters, and people for whom English is not a primary language — the populations most likely to be affected by water-quality issues and least likely to be served by technical regulatory documents. A beautiful explainer that leaves the reader with a false sense of certainty because they could not read it is a failure.
objective: Harden the web app (built on wqe-web-006 and wqe-engine-008) to WCAG 2.2 AA: (1) run automated axe/pa11y scan with zero critical violations; (2) complete manual AT audit on the defined matrix (NVDA+Firefox, JAWS+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari, TalkBack+Chrome, keyboard-only) and document findings; (3) meet and enforce a plain-language readability target in CI (Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent metric); (4) ensure explainer output meets both gates before M1 exit.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated axe/pa11y scan passes with zero critical violations on all explainer and lookup pages
Manual AT audit completed on the defined matrix: NVDA+Firefox, JAWS+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari, TalkBack+Chrome, and keyboard-only navigation
Manual AT audit findings documented and any blocking issues resolved before M1 exit
Plain-language readability target (Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent) met and enforced in CI; CI fails if target is not met
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR with a11y fixes, automated a11y test configuration (axe/pa11y in CI), plain-language readability CI check, and a manual AT audit report committed to the repo.
